name: Virginia
address: 1300 East Main Street, 1st Floor, Richmond, VA 23219

llc.formName: Articles of Cancellation of a Virginia Limited Liability Company
steps: Vote to dissolve the LLC according to the operating agreement or by member consent
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final state tax returns with the Virginia Department of Taxation
Complete Form LLC-1050 — Articles of Cancellation
File Form LLC-1050 with the State Corporation Commission with $25 filing fee
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
llc.notes: Virginia uses the State Corporation Commission (SCC), not a Secretary of State, for business filings. The filing fee is $25. You can file online at cis.scc.virginia.gov and pay with credit card or eCheck. By mail, send to P.O. Box 1197, Richmond, VA 23218.

corporation.formName: Articles of Dissolution of a Virginia Stock Corporation
steps: Hold a board of directors meeting to recommend dissolution
Obtain shareholder approval for the dissolution
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final state tax returns with the Virginia Department of Taxation
Complete Form SCC-743 — Articles of Dissolution
File Form SCC-743 with the State Corporation Commission with $25 filing fee
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
corporation.notes: Virginia uses the State Corporation Commission (SCC), not a Secretary of State. The filing fee is $25. You can file online through the Clerk's Information System (CIS) at cis.scc.virginia.gov. For termination after dissolution, use Form SCC-750 (Articles of Termination).

tax.notes: Virginia does not require a formal tax clearance certificate for dissolution. However, you must file final income tax returns and settle any outstanding tax obligations. You should also complete the winding-up process and pay any outstanding debts and taxes before filing for dissolution.

requirements.publicationDetails: Corporations must publish notice of dissolution one time in a newspaper of general circulation in the city or county of the principal/registered office, or post conspicuously for at least 30 days on the dissolved corporation's website.
requirements.employeeNotification: Must provide WARN Act notice (60 days) if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors of the dissolution and provide a reasonable deadline for claims (minimum 120 days)
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent designation terminates upon dissolution
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Must complete winding up of all business affairs before filing articles of cancellation or dissolution
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLC: Member vote as specified in operating agreement. Corporation: Board resolution followed by shareholder approval (more than two-thirds of shares entitled to vote).

includes: Every required form with direct download links
